seo.title: About Beth Motley, MD, FACLM — Lifestyle Medicine Physician
seo.description: Learn about Dr. Motley's journey from biomedical engineering to lifestyle medicine. Board-certified family and lifestyle medicine physician in Greenville, SC.

hero.headline: Physician. Educator. Advocate.
hero.subheadline: Combining an engineer's analytical rigor with a physician's compassion, Dr. Motley helps patients use the most powerful medicine available: the way they live.

story.imageAlt: Beth Motley, MD, FACLM — Lifestyle Medicine Physician
story.title: From Engineering to Medicine
paragraphs: Dr. Motley did not take the typical path to medicine. She began her career studying biomedical engineering at the University of Virginia, where she learned to approach complex systems with precision, data, and a relentless focus on outcomes. That analytical foundation would become one of her greatest strengths as a physician.

During her medical training at EVMS / Old Dominion University, she noticed a pattern that troubled her: patients with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and heart disease were being managed with escalating medications, but the underlying causes — poor nutrition, inactivity, chronic stress, inadequate sleep — were rarely addressed in any meaningful way. The standard model treated symptoms. She wanted to treat the source.

That realization led her to lifestyle medicine, a rapidly growing medical specialty built on a straightforward premise: the leading causes of chronic disease are largely driven by lifestyle, and they can be reversed by changing it. The evidence was compelling, the outcomes were measurable, and the approach aligned perfectly with her engineering instincts — identify the root cause, intervene at the source, and measure the results.

After completing her family medicine residency at Prisma Health in Greenville, SC, Dr. Motley built a practice that integrates traditional primary care with intensive lifestyle interventions. Her Diabetes Reversal Shared Medical Appointments bring together small groups of patients who share a common diagnosis and walk through a structured curriculum together, combining medical care, education, and group discussion in each session.

The results have been striking. Patients in her program routinely lower their A1C, reduce or eliminate medications, lose weight, and report dramatic improvements in energy and quality of life. She is a Fellow of the American College of Lifestyle Medicine and is committed to advancing the field through clinical practice, medical education, and public speaking.

credentials.title: Education & Credentials
credentials.subtitle: A rigorous academic foundation paired with advanced training in lifestyle medicine.

timeline.title: BS Biomedical Engineering
timeline.description: Developed a foundation in systems thinking, data analysis, and evidence-based problem solving that would later shape her clinical approach.

timeline.title: Doctor of Medicine
timeline.description: Trained in a community-oriented medical school with a strong emphasis on primary care, clinical reasoning, and patient-centered communication.

timeline.title: Family Medicine Residency
timeline.institution: Prisma Health / University of South Carolina Greenville
timeline.description: Completed residency training in full-spectrum family medicine, building expertise across inpatient, outpatient, and procedural care.

timeline.title: Family & Lifestyle Medicine Physician
timeline.description: Practices both family and lifestyle medicine, integrating evidence-based behavioral interventions into primary care to help patients reverse chronic disease.

timeline.title: Fellow, American College of Lifestyle Medicine
timeline.description: Recognized by the ACLM for demonstrated expertise and commitment to the practice and advancement of lifestyle medicine.

approach.title: My Approach
approach.subtitle: Three principles guide every patient interaction, program design, and clinical decision.

items.title: Evidence-Based
items.description: Every recommendation is grounded in peer-reviewed research and aligned with the American College of Lifestyle Medicine clinical guidelines. No fads, no guesswork — just science that has been validated in clinical trials and real-world practice.

items.title: Patient-Centered
items.description: Dr. Motley leads shared medical appointments that combine medical care, education, and group discussion. This approach empowers patients to take ownership of their health rather than passively receiving instructions.

items.title: Whole-Person
items.description: Rather than treating symptoms in isolation, Dr. Motley addresses the root causes of chronic disease across all six pillars of lifestyle medicine. The goal is not just better lab values, but a fundamentally healthier, more resilient life.

cta.title: Ready to Get Started?
cta.description: Whether you are looking to reverse a chronic condition or bring evidence-based health education to your audience, Dr. Motley is here to help.

links.title: Explore Programs
links.description: Learn about the Diabetes Reversal and Executive MD programs.

links.title: Book Dr. Motley to Speak
links.description: Invite Dr. Motley to your event, podcast, or conference.
